name: Panama City — Bella Vista, Panama

visa.notes: Pensionado visa requires $1,000/mo pension income. Extensive retiree discounts.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: World-class private hospitals including Johns Hopkins-affiliated Punta Pacifica Hospital. Public CSS available.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private facilities; moderate at public

pros: Modern urban amenities and world-class dining
Excellent private healthcare infrastructure
Major international hub with direct flights
cons: Higher cost of living than other Panama locations

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income. Panama City has higher living costs but extensive urban amenities.

name: Panama City — El Cangrejo, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Access to Panama City's private hospital network. Multiple clinics nearby.

pros: Central location with diverse dining and nightlife
More affordable than Bella Vista or Punta Pacifica
Walkable neighborhood with metro access

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Panama City — Casco Viejo, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Access to Panama City's private hospital network.

pros: UNESCO World Heritage historic district
Trendy restaurants, rooftop bars, and cultural venues
Walkable cobblestone streets with ocean views

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Panama City — Punta Pacifica, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Adjacent to Johns Hopkins-affiliated Punta Pacifica Hospital. Top-tier private care.

pros: Upscale oceanfront high-rises with stunning views
Walking distance to top hospital
Modern amenities, pools, gyms in buildings

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Panama City — Costa del Este, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Access to Panama City's private hospital network. Pacifica Salud nearby.

pros: Planned suburban community with parks and green spaces
Family-friendly with modern shopping centers
Newer construction with good building standards

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Coronado, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Local clinics; Panama City hospitals 1.5 hours away. San Fernando clinic in Coronado.
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate locally; travel needed for specialists
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Basic availability locally; full range in Panama City

pros: Pacific beach lifestyle with established expat community
Gated communities with amenities and golf
Reasonably close to Panama City for services
cons: Beach can be rough for swimming

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: El Valle de Antón, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Basic local clinic only. Panama City hospitals 2 hours away.
healthcare.waitTimes: Limited local options; travel required for most care
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Basic pharmacy locally; full range in Panama City

pros: Unique volcanic crater setting with cooler climate
Affordable mountain living
Famous Sunday market and natural hot springs
cons: Very small town with limited services

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Chitré, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Regional hospital in Chitré. Specialists limited; Panama City for complex care.

pros: Driest region in Panama (Azuero Peninsula)
Very affordable cost of living
Authentic Panamanian culture and festivals

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: David, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Hospital Chiriquí and Mae Lewis regional hospitals. Best healthcare hub outside Panama City.
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate; good for a regional city

pros: Regional hub with shopping, airport, and hospitals
Gateway to Boquete highlands (30 min drive)
Affordable with urban conveniences
cons: Not as scenic as highland or beach alternatives

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Pedasí, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Small local clinic. Chitré hospital 45 min away; Panama City for specialists.

pros: Quiet Pacific coast fishing village charm
Excellent surfing and whale watching
Very affordable with low crime
cons: Very small town with minimal services

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Volcán, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Basic local clinic. David hospitals 45 min away.
healthcare.waitTimes: Limited local care; David for most needs

pros: Cool highland climate near Barú volcano
Very affordable and peaceful
Rich agricultural area with fresh produce
cons: Less developed expat infrastructure than Boquete

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Bocas del Toro, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Basic island clinic. Mainland hospitals in Changuinola or David for serious care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Very limited; boat/flight to mainland for specialists

pros: Caribbean island paradise with turquoise waters
Laid-back beach lifestyle
Growing expat and digital nomad community
cons: Isolated island location limits healthcare access

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Puerto Armuelles, Panama

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Basic local clinic. David hospitals 1.5 hours away.

pros: Extremely affordable Pacific coast living
Beachfront property still available at low cost
Small but friendly expat community
cons: Former banana company town still recovering economically
Remote location far from major amenities

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income.

name: Central Valley (San José, Escazú, Santa Ana), Costa Rica

visa.notes: Pensionado visa requires $1,000/mo pension or retirement income. Must spend at least 4 months/year in Costa Rica.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CAJA (public universal healthcare) plus excellent private hospitals (CIMA, Clínica Bíblica). Among best in Latin America.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private; moderate at CAJA public system
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: CAJA covers prescriptions; private insurance also available

pros: Best healthcare infrastructure in Central America
Spring-like climate year-round in the valley
Large established expat community in Escazú/Santa Ana
cons: Traffic congestion in San José metro area
Higher cost of living than other Costa Rica areas
Air quality issues in urban core

taxes.notes: Territorial tax system: only Costa Rica-sourced income is taxed. Foreign pensions and Social Security not taxed. CAJA mandatory contribution ~11% of declared income.

name: Guanacaste (Tamarindo, Nosara, Liberia), Costa Rica

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CAJA public system; Liberia has regional hospital. Private clinics in beach towns for basic care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate; San José for complex procedures
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Available through CAJA; private pharmacies in towns

pros: Driest region with beautiful Pacific beaches
Strong surf/yoga/wellness culture
International airport in Liberia for easy access
cons: Tourist prices in Tamarindo and Nosara
Car essential; roads can be rough in rainy season

taxes.notes: Territorial tax system: foreign income not taxed.

name: Arenal / Lake Arenal, Costa Rica

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CAJA clinics in La Fortuna; San José for specialists (2.5 hours).
healthcare.waitTimes: Limited locally; travel to San José for complex care

pros: Stunning volcanic lake scenery
Established and growing expat community
Affordable with cooler highland climate

taxes.notes: Territorial tax system: foreign income not taxed.

name: Puerto Viejo, Costa Rica

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CAJA clinic locally; Limón hospital 1 hour. San José 4+ hours for specialists.

pros: Beautiful Caribbean beaches and reggae culture
Laid-back, bike-friendly coastal town
Diverse international community
cons: Petty theft can be an issue

taxes.notes: Territorial tax system: foreign income not taxed.

name: Atenas, Costa Rica

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CAJA clinic locally; San José hospitals 45 minutes away.
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate locally; good access to San José

pros: Claimed 'best climate in the world' by National Geographic
Close to San José for healthcare and shopping
Established expat community in small-town setting

taxes.notes: Territorial tax system: foreign income not taxed.

name: Grecia, Costa Rica

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: CAJA clinic locally; San José hospitals under 1 hour.

pros: Excellent spring-like climate similar to Atenas
More affordable than Atenas or Escazú
Famous red metal church and charming town center
cons: Smaller expat community than other CR locations
Limited English spoken outside expat circles
Small-town pace may not suit everyone

taxes.notes: Territorial tax system: foreign income not taxed.

name: Lake Chapala / Ajijic, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 in savings. Renewable annually, converts to permanent after 4 years.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS public system; excellent private hospitals in Guadalajara (45 min). Local clinics in Ajijic.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at local private clinics; Guadalajara for specialists
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Very affordable; many meds available over the counter

pros: Largest US/Canadian expat community in Mexico
Spring-like climate year-round at lake elevation
Extremely well-established expat infrastructure
cons: Can feel like an American bubble

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents: only Mexican-source income taxed. US pensions and Social Security not taxed by Mexico for temporary residents.

name: San Miguel de Allende, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 savings.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Local private clinics and hospitals. Querétaro (1.5 hours) for advanced care.

pros: UNESCO World Heritage colonial architecture
Vibrant arts, culture, and food scene
Large welcoming expat community
cons: Winter nights can be cold (no central heating)
Prices inflated by tourism and expat demand
Cobblestone streets difficult for mobility issues

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents. Foreign income not taxed.

name: Mérida, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 savings.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS public system; several good private hospitals (Star Médica, Centro Médico de las Américas).

pros: One of the safest cities in Mexico
Rich Mayan culture and excellent cuisine
Affordable with good infrastructure
cons: Less English spoken than other expat hubs

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents. Foreign income not taxed.

name: Puerto Vallarta, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 savings.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS public system; several private hospitals (CMQ, San Javier). Medical tourism hub.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private facilities; moderate at IMSS

pros: Beautiful Pacific beach with mountain backdrop
LGBTQ+-friendly with vibrant social scene
Excellent dining and nightlife
cons: Hot and humid summers with heavy rain

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents. Foreign income not taxed.

name: Playa del Carmen, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 savings.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS available; private hospitals in Cancún (1 hour). Growing local private clinics.

pros: Caribbean beaches with turquoise water
International community and diverse dining
Convenient to Cancún airport for US flights
cons: Rising security concerns in Quintana Roo

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents. Foreign income not taxed.

name: Oaxaca City, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 savings.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS public system; limited private hospitals. Mexico City for complex care.

pros: World-renowned cuisine and mezcal culture
Rich indigenous heritage and artisan traditions
Very affordable cost of living
cons: Periodic political protests and road blockades

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents. Foreign income not taxed.

name: Querétaro, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 savings.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS public system; good private hospitals. Mexico City 2.5 hours for top-tier care.

pros: One of Mexico's safest and fastest-growing cities
Modern infrastructure with colonial centro
Growing tech/business hub with good jobs
cons: Smaller expat community than Chapala or SMA
Less tourist infrastructure than beach towns

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents. Foreign income not taxed.

name: Mazatlán, Mexico

visa.notes: Temporary resident visa requires ~$1,500/mo income or ~$25,000 savings.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IMSS public system; Sharp Mazatlán private hospital. Guadalajara for complex care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private; moderate at IMSS

pros: Affordable Pacific beach city with long malecón
Renovated historic centro with cultural revival
Growing expat community with authentic Mexican feel
cons: Sinaloa state has security reputation issues

taxes.notes: Territorial tax for temporary residents. Foreign income not taxed.

name: Medellín (El Poblado, Laureles), Colombia

visa.notes: Retirement visa requires ~$750/mo pension income (3x minimum wage). Renewable annually. Colombia also offers a digital nomad visa.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: EPS public system; excellent private hospitals. Medellín is a medical tourism hub with top-rated clinics.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private hospitals; moderate at EPS

pros: Spring-like climate year-round ('City of Eternal Spring')
World-class healthcare at fraction of US cost
Large and growing expat community
cons: Expat reputation issues in some areas (El Poblado)
Language barrier—Spanish essential outside expat zones

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income if not a tax resident (under 183 days). If tax resident, worldwide income is taxed at progressive rates up to 39%.

name: Pereira / Coffee Axis, Colombia

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: EPS public system; regional hospitals in Pereira. Medellín or Bogotá for complex care.

pros: UNESCO Coffee Cultural Landscape
Very affordable cost of living
Pleasant spring-like climate in coffee region

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income if under 183 days/year.

name: Cartagena, Colombia

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: EPS public system; good private clinics. Growing medical tourism sector.

pros: Stunning UNESCO colonial walled city
Caribbean beach lifestyle
Growing international community
cons: Tourist area prices can be high
Aggressive street vendors in tourist zones

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income if under 183 days/year.

name: Santa Marta, Colombia

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: EPS public system; regional hospital. Barranquilla for specialists.

pros: Affordable Caribbean beach city
Gateway to Tayrona National Park
Less touristy than Cartagena
cons: Infrastructure less developed than other cities

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income if under 183 days/year.

name: Bogotá (Chapinero, Usaquén), Colombia

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: EPS public system; Bogotá has Colombia's best hospitals (Fundación Santa Fe, Clínica del Country).

pros: Cosmopolitan capital with world-class dining and culture
Best healthcare in Colombia
Affordable compared to major world capitals
cons: Cool and rainy climate (8,600 ft elevation)

taxes.notes: No tax on foreign-source income if under 183 days/year.

name: Cuenca, Ecuador

visa.notes: Retirement visa requires $1,375/mo income (3x Ecuador minimum wage). Includes discounts on utilities, flights, and entertainment.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IESS public system; good private hospitals (Hospital Monte Sinaí). Affordable private care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Short at private; moderate at IESS public
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Affordable; many meds available without prescription

pros: Large established expat community
UNESCO World Heritage colonial city
Very affordable with USD currency (no exchange risk)
cons: Altitude (8,400 ft) may cause adjustment issues
Cool climate may not suit warm-weather seekers
Political instability and occasional unrest in Ecuador

taxes.notes: Ecuador uses USD. Income tax on worldwide income if fiscal resident, but retiree visa holders may qualify for exemptions. Progressive rates 0-37%. Jubilado discount benefits offset many costs.

name: Quito, Ecuador

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: IESS public system; best hospitals in Ecuador (Hospital Metropolitano, Clínica Pasteur).

pros: Capital city with best healthcare and services
UNESCO World Heritage historic center
Uses USD with no exchange rate risk
cons: High altitude (9,350 ft) causes adjustment issues

taxes.notes: Ecuador uses USD. Progressive income tax 0-37% on worldwide income for residents.

name: Cotacachi, Ecuador

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Basic local clinic. Ibarra (30 min) or Quito (2 hours) for hospital care.

pros: Extremely affordable highland living
Famous leather goods market
Small but established expat community
cons: Very small town with limited services

taxes.notes: Ecuador uses USD. Jubilado discounts apply.

name: Vilcabamba, Ecuador

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Very basic local clinic. Loja (1 hour) for hospital. Quito/Guayaquil for specialists.

pros: Known as 'Valley of Longevity' with pleasant climate
Extremely affordable cost of living
Peaceful rural setting with organic food culture

taxes.notes: Ecuador uses USD. Jubilado discounts apply.

name: Salinas, Ecuador

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Local clinics; Guayaquil (2.5 hours) for hospital care.
healthcare.waitTimes: Limited locally; travel to Guayaquil for specialists

pros: Warm coastal beach town with very little rain
Uses USD with no exchange risk
Popular with Ecuadorian and expat retirees
cons: Can feel isolated from major services

taxes.notes: Ecuador uses USD. Jubilado discounts apply.

name: Montevideo, Uruguay

visa.notes: Proof of stable income ~$1,500/mo required. Uruguay offers straightforward residency with path to citizenship.

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: FONASA public system; good private mutualistas (Hospital Británico, Médica Uruguaya). Quality comparable to developed nations.
healthcare.waitTimes: Moderate at public; short at private mutualistas
healthcare.prescriptionCoverage: Good coverage through mutualistas; pharmacies common

pros: Most stable democracy in South America
High quality of life with European feel
Good healthcare system and infrastructure
cons: Higher cost of living than most Latin American options

taxes.notes: Foreign income tax-exempt in year 1. After that, foreign income taxed at flat 12% (reduced from standard rates). Uruguay has 22% IVA (VAT).

name: Punta del Este, Uruguay

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Local clinics; Montevideo hospitals 1.5 hours for complex care.

pros: Upscale resort town with beautiful beaches
Attracts international crowd (Argentine, Brazilian, European)
Very safe with high quality of life
cons: Very seasonal—dead in winter, expensive in summer
One of the most expensive places in South America

taxes.notes: Foreign income tax-exempt year 1, then 12% flat rate on foreign income.

name: Colonia del Sacramento, Uruguay

medicine.notes: Monthly prescription medication costs for household

healthcare.system: Local clinics; Montevideo hospitals 2.5 hours. Buenos Aires ferry 1 hour.
healthcare.waitTimes: Limited locally; travel to Montevideo or Buenos Aires

pros: UNESCO World Heritage charming colonial town
Affordable by Uruguay standards
Ferry access to Buenos Aires for services and culture

taxes.notes: Foreign income tax-exempt year 1, then 12% flat rate on foreign income.
